llm_result.summary: Case presents with: (C01) auditory trigger sensitivity to chewing sounds, (C02) intense but potentially controllable anger, (C03) perseverative attention / inability to habituate to trigger sounds, (C04) self-directed distress about these reactions, and (C05) co-occurring texture sensitivity. The following modifiable predictors span BIO, PSYCHO, and SOCIAL domains and their interactions. Each lever is designed to be changeable, non-diagnostic, and safe, with clear measurement and a feasible path to impact within weeks to months. The plan emphasizes sensory environment optimization, emotion and attention regulation skills, sensory-texture processing strategies, social support, and data-driven personalization to move the specified criteria toward improvement. Safety notes emphasize monitoring for escalation and seeking professional help if risk emerges.

solutions.predictor_variable: Auditory environment optimization (noise-cancelling devices, masking sounds, seating/adjacency adjustments)
solutions.solution_candidate: Environmental_Sensory_Environment_Optimization: implement access to high-quality noise-cancelling headphones, calibrated masking sounds (e.g., white/noise or ambient sound mixes), and deliberate seating/place selection to minimize proximity to likely chewing-noise sources; pair with a structured sensory-friendly room/space protocol and gradual exposure plan.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces salience of chewing-triggered sounds and reduces automatic attentional capture, facilitating quicker disengagement and habituation, thereby lowering aversive reactivity and intrusive replay of sounds.
solutions.measurement: Daily trigger exposure log; 0-10 anger rating post-exposure; number and duration of intrusive sound recollections; self-reported comfort with environment (0-100 scale).
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure safe use of devices (volume limits); avoid isolation that reduces real-world exposure without plan; monitor for decreased social engagement if over-isolation occurs.
solutions.mapping_query: Map to C01 and C03 by showing how reduced trigger salience and less persistent attention to chewing sounds translates into lower aversive reactivity and diminished perseverative auditory processing.

solutions.solution_candidate: Emotion_Regulation_Skills_Training: structured program teaching identification of emotions, cognitive reappraisal, distress tolerance, and adaptive coping (brief, app-supported or in-session).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhanced ability to regulate sudden anger (C02) and reduce self-criticism and distress about reactions (C04) by reframing trigger interpretations and deploying coping strategies.
solutions.measurement: Anger intensity ratings (0-10) after triggers; frequency/severity of self-criticism related to sensory reactions (Likert items); use of emotion-regulation strategies captured via brief daily check-ins.
solutions.safety_notes: No pharmacology; ensure skills are practiced with guidance; monitor for temporary frustration during new strategies.
solutions.mapping_query: Link improvements in C02 and C04 to greater emotion control and reduced self-criticism through explicit emotion regulation use.

solutions.predictor_variable: Attention control training / habituation-based cognitive interventions
solutions.solution_candidate: Attention_Control_Training_and_Habituation: use computational/behavioral training to strengthen top-down attention control and reduce perseverative cognition about trigger sounds; incorporate graded exposure elements with inhibitory learning principles.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Strengthened attentional control reduces the persistence of trigger-related thoughts and sensory intrusion (C03) and may reduce the perceived salience of triggers (C01) over time.
solutions.measurement: Frequency/drequency of trigger-related intrusions; reaction time to shift attention after exposure; daily cueing logs for attentional attempts.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor frustration or fatigue; pace tasks to avoid overwhelm.
solutions.mapping_query: Demonstrate how improved attention control and habituation reduce perseverative attention (C03) and lower trigger salience (C01).

solutions.solution_candidate: Self-Compassion and Self-Relating Training: cultivate kinder self-talk and reduce self-criticism about sensory reactions; integrate with cognitive-behavioral reframing and acceptance-based strategies.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Decreases self-directed distress (C04) and attenuates anger intensity (C02) by reducing punitive self-judgment and reorienting responses toward curiosity and acceptance.
solutions.measurement: Self-Compassion Scale (SCS) scores; self-criticism intensity related to sensory reactions; daily mood logs.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure supportive framing to avoid triggering guilt/shame spirals; adjust pace if self-judgment worsens.
solutions.mapping_query: Show how increased self-compassion lowers C04 distress and modulates C02 anger reactivity.

solutions.predictor_variable: Texture processing strategies and sensory-texture exposure
solutions.solution_candidate: Texture_Sensory_Integration_Strategy: graded tactile exposure with safe, controlled textures; sensory-texture mindfulness exercises; environmental adaptations to reduce texture-provoked discomfort.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Directly targets texture sensitivity (C05) and may indirectly dampen overall sensory reactivity by desensitizing cross-modal triggers and reducing overall arousal.
solutions.measurement: Texture sensitivity rating scale (0-10) after each session; log of avoidance behaviors; qualitative notes on texture tolerance progression.
solutions.safety_notes: Avoid overwhelming stimuli; proceed with professional guidance if sensory distress escalates; monitor signs of sensory overload.
solutions.mapping_query: Link C05 improvement to structured tactile exposure and sensory integration principles.

solutions.predictor_variable: Gut-brain axis support through nutrition and microbiome-focused patterns
solutions.solution_candidate: Nutrition_GutBrain_Axis_Support: optimize dietary patterns (high fiber, diverse plant-based foods, probiotic-rich options) and consistent hydration to support neuroinflammation modulation and neurotransmitter balance; avoid triggers that worsen GI symptoms.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Modulates systemic inflammation and gut-brain signaling which can influence sensory processing and emotional reactivity, potentially reducing C01 sensitivity and C05 texture-linked dysregulation.
solutions.measurement: Food diary with trigger logging; GI symptom tracking; 0-10 ratings of trigger salience; texture sensitivity logging.
solutions.safety_notes: Consult nutritionist if medical conditions exist; do not treat as medical advice; monitor for GI intolerance.
solutions.mapping_query: Map nutrition-related gut-brain changes to reductions in C01 and C05.

solutions.predictor_variable: Personalization, monitoring, and data feedback loop
solutions.solution_candidate: Personalization_Monitoring_and_Data_Feedback: implement self-monitoring tools (diaries, brief daily scales, optional wearables) with periodic feedback to tailor interventions across BIO/PSYCHO/SOCIAL domains.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enables dynamic adjustment of interventions based on real-time data, promoting adherence and targeted impact across all five criteria.
solutions.measurement: Composite weekly progress report; adherence metrics; variability in symptom measures; dashboard-based feedback satisfaction.
solutions.safety_notes: Protect privacy; ensure data security; provide opt-out options for sensitive data.
solutions.mapping_query: Demonstrate how continuous monitoring and feedback optimize multi-domain targets (C01-C05).

solutions.solution_candidate: Care_Access_and_Social_Support_Optimization: strengthen access to care, connect with supportive networks, and incorporate social resources to reduce distress related to sensory reactions.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhanced social support and care access reduce distress and self-criticism related to sensory reactions by提供 validation, coping resources, and accountability.
solutions.measurement: Number of supportive contacts per week; access to therapy or coaching sessions; perceived social support scale; distress about reactions (C04) rating.
solutions.safety_notes: Safeguard privacy; coordinate with family or caregivers with consent; monitor for boundary issues.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S08 to C04 via improved social support and care access reducing self-directed distress.
